title: Cutting Parameters Tool Life Calculator
description: Calculates tool life based on cutting speed, feed rate, depth of cut, and material properties, with adjustments for process reliability and hidden loss drivers.

inputs.label: Cutting Speed
inputs.businessContext: Surface speed of the workpiece relative to the cutting tool, based on ISO 3685.

businessContext_i18n.en: Surface speed of the workpiece relative to the cutting tool, based on ISO 3685.

inputs.label: Feed Rate
inputs.businessContext: Linear distance the tool advances per revolution, affects chip load and tool wear.

businessContext_i18n.en: Linear distance the tool advances per revolution, affects chip load and tool wear.

inputs.label: Depth of Cut
inputs.businessContext: Radial or axial engagement of the tool, influences cutting forces and heat generation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Radial or axial engagement of the tool, influences cutting forces and heat generation.

inputs.label: Tool Material
inputs.businessContext: Material of the cutting tool, affects wear resistance and optimal cutting parameters.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material of the cutting tool, affects wear resistance and optimal cutting parameters.

inputs.label: Workpiece Material
inputs.businessContext: Material being machined, determines machinability rating and recommended speeds.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material being machined, determines machinability rating and recommended speeds.

inputs.label: Coolant Used
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if coolant is applied, reduces thermal stress and extends tool life.

businessContext_i18n.en: Indicates if coolant is applied, reduces thermal stress and extends tool life.

inputs.label: Machine Stability Factor
inputs.businessContext: Factor representing machine rigidity and vibration damping, based on condition monitoring data.

businessContext_i18n.en: Factor representing machine rigidity and vibration damping, based on condition monitoring data.

rules.message: Cutting speed to feed rate ratio is too low, risk of built-up edge formation.
Depth of cut exceeds recommended limit for superalloys, tool failure likely.
Ceramic tools should not be used with coolant due to thermal shock risk.

thresholds.message: Tool life is critically low (<10 minutes), consider reducing cutting speed or feed.
Immediate action required: tool life below 5 minutes, stop machining and review parameters.
